rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FLORENCE CHANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although neither proof of service filed on 1/18/18 or 2/16/18 had the minor beneficiary listed separately and a corrected, complete proof of service was not filed to correct the error (only a purportedly missing page of the service list was attached to the supplement filed on 6/12/18). A Notice of Hearing is never appropriate for the initial Petition for Probate and no notice is required for a continuance in any event unless an amending pleading is filed. Status of closing estate set for 7/31/19.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Assets Were Effectively Transferred To The Otellini Family Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/14/18 - a parent cannot waive notice for a minor. Please file a Supplement addressing CRC 7.51(d) and give direct notice to each minor and to the person(s) having legal custody of each minor with whom that minor resides. Once proper notice has been given, an appearance will be required re transfer of assets to Trust.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RODNEY BRUCE MIDDLETON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required for the court to determine whether to grant the petition. although the petitioner is nominated by one of the two heirs, he is also making a personal claim to 100% of the estate. Counsel may be asked to address Petitioner's ability to impartially administer the estate. In addition, when a PR makes a personal claim to a probate estate as against the heirs, this court requires the PR to have separate counsel to pursue that claim. Counsel may be asked to address in which capacity he will be representing Petitioner.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AUDREY YATES
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: continued to july 24, 2018. new publication is required, as probate code section 8121 states the first publication must be 15 days before the hearing, and the proof of publication shows the first publication was 6/19, only 14 days before 7/3 (skip the first, count the last). in addition, no proposed order delivered to the examiner (please note, FSX does NOT deliver lodged orders to the court.
